description: Baseline for make agent-ci on a stable tiny scripts/ diff; end-to-end time is dominated by the routed ci-fast gate, while classifier-only cost is small but measurable.

asi.hypothesis: Establish a stable benchmark before optimizing routing overhead.
asi.workload: make agent-ci with a persistent tracked probe line in scripts/agent-ci-surface.sh causing ci-fast routing
asi.baseline_observation: Classifier overhead (~75ms) is tiny relative to full ci-fast runtime (~100s). Pure classifier optimization may not materially improve the primary metric unless routing target or gate startup cost also changes.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for ways to reduce ci-fast startup/work under this surface without changing routing semantics, or retarget to a more routing-dominated workload if the goal is strictly classifier speed.

description: Tried adding a script-surface-specific ci-fast branch, but it broke the documented outside-git release-gate fallback and the benchmark harness was masking command failures.

asi.hypothesis: A narrow contract-only verification path for CI-router shell scripts could cut ci-fast time without weakening safety.
asi.rollback_reason: The new ci-fast shell branch used git path discovery inside non-git fallback flows, causing agent-ci contract failure outside git; autoresearch harness also mis-recorded nonzero exit codes due to `if ! cmd` misuse.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert the ci-fast branch, fix the harness failure capture, then explore optimizations that preserve non-git fallback behavior or benchmark a narrower command directly.
asi.lesson: Any agent-ci optimization must preserve the source-snapshot/no-git fallback path documented in ci-strategy and tested by release integration contracts.

description: Control rerun after fixing autoresearch harness failure capture; warm-cache ci-fast runtime is far lower than the original baseline, showing large session noise.

asi.hypothesis: Measure benchmark noise after fixing status capture in autoresearch.sh.
asi.workload: Unchanged repository logic; only benchmark harness failure capture fixed
asi.baseline_observation: Warm-cache agent-ci runs can vary by tens of seconds while classifier time stays ~70-80ms, so confidence on small optimization wins will require repeated controls.
asi.next_action_hint: Run at least one more unchanged control to estimate the noise floor before trusting any marginal improvement in agent_ci_ms.

description: Repeated unchanged control run to measure noise; agent-ci swung back to ~101s, confirming the workload is too noisy for trusting single-run wins.

asi.hypothesis: Estimate the noise floor of the unchanged agent-ci workload.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; control run was worse than baseline and confirms large variance dominated by test/lint execution.
asi.next_action_hint: Either redesign autoresearch.sh to benchmark a lower-noise subcommand such as the classifier itself, or report that end-to-end make agent-ci is dominated by noisy validation phases unsuitable for fine-grained optimization without repeated medians.
asi.noise_observation: Observed unchanged controls at ~62s and ~101s with classifier steady near ~80ms.

description: Retargeted the experiment to the real classifier overhead and updated the harness to use a 9-sample median for scripts/agent-ci-surface.sh --target on the stable tiny-diff workload.

asi.hypothesis: A lower-noise classifier-only metric is the right optimization target for this session; end-to-end agent-ci remains a secondary guardrail only.
asi.workload: Median of 9 classifier runs plus one guardrail make agent-ci run on the stable scripts/ diff workload
asi.benchmark_fix: Re-initialized autoresearch to use classifier_ms as the primary metric and documented why the prior end-to-end metric was too noisy.
asi.next_action_hint: Now optimize scripts/agent-ci-surface.sh and release_policy helper usage directly, validating against the stable classifier metric while watching end-to-end agent_ci_ms for pathological regressions.

description: Tried replacing changed-path collection with one git status porcelain parse, but the new parser broke macOS/app routing contracts and failed agent-ci validation.

asi.hypothesis: One git status --porcelain=v1 -z call plus local parsing could replace three Git subprocesses and cut classifier_ms.
asi.rollback_reason: The parser changed routing semantics: app and mk/macos surfaces incorrectly downgraded to ci-fast, so the change is not acceptable even if faster.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the existing multi-command collection for correctness, or prototype a parser only with stronger fixture coverage before rerunning.
asi.lesson: Classifier micro-optimizations must preserve all path-shape semantics exactly; contract tests catch subtle parse drift quickly.

description: Replaced repeated target-rank shell comparisons with direct case-based monotonic candidate promotion in scripts/agent-ci-surface.sh, yielding a tiny classifier median improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating target_rank helper calls and command substitutions in the hot candidate-promotion path can shave a small amount of classifier overhead without changing semantics.
asi.practical_significance: Very small improvement (81ms -> 80ms); likely near noise floor, but correctness checks passed and code is simpler in the hot path.
asi.next_action_hint: Look next at changed_paths collection and Makefile diff special-case helpers for larger wins, but preserve contract coverage and no-git behavior.

description: Inlined the docs-only path case match in scripts/agent-ci-surface.sh instead of calling public_is_docs_only_path for each changed path, improving classifier median time.

asi.hypothesis: Removing per-path shell function dispatch in the docs-only scan can reduce classifier overhead while preserving the exact docs-only policy.
asi.practical_significance: Small but clearer win than the previous iteration (80ms -> 78ms) with unchanged routing semantics and passing checks.
asi.next_action_hint: Next likely wins are other per-path helper calls in classify_special_path or reducing repeated Makefile diff helper work without changing policy.

description: Inlined script-path release/macOS routing matches directly in scripts/agent-ci-surface.sh instead of calling release_policy helper functions for each scripts/* path, materially reducing classifier median time.

asi.hypothesis: Inlining stable exact-match script routing policy locally can remove cross-file shell function dispatch overhead in classify_special_path without changing semantics.
asi.practical_significance: This is the clearest improvement so far (78ms -> 71ms) while preserving routing behavior and passing checks.
asi.next_action_hint: Next likely win is reducing repeated helper dispatch for non-script path checks or caching Makefile diff text once when Makefile is in changed_paths.

description: Tried caching Makefile diff text once for Makefile-specific routing checks, but the added pre-check overhead regressed the classifier on the current small scripts/ workload.

asi.hypothesis: Computing Makefile diff lines once and reusing them would reduce repeated work when Makefile is in the changed set.
asi.rollback_reason: On the benchmark workload Makefile is not changed, so the unconditional membership check added overhead without offsetting savings.
asi.next_action_hint: Prefer optimizations that help the dominant scripts/ workload directly, or only add Makefile caching when the script is already inside a Makefile-specific branch.
asi.lesson: Pre-optimization guards can cost more than the saved work when the guarded path is rare in the benchmarked workload.

description: Tried moving scripts/* special-path routing ahead of Makefile handling in classify_special_path, but it regressed classifier median time on the benchmark workload.

asi.hypothesis: Putting the dominant scripts/* workload first in classify_special_path would reduce branch work for the active benchmark case.
asi.rollback_reason: The reordering did not improve the classifier and likely just shifted branch/layout costs without reducing meaningful work.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus next on helper calls that still occur per path in the main classification loop, especially non-script path helper dispatch, instead of pure branch reordering.
asi.lesson: Branch reordering alone is not a reliable win when the remaining work is dominated by helper calls and external command cost.

description: Inlined non-script path routing case matches in the main classification loop, but the result still regressed versus the current best classifier implementation.

asi.hypothesis: Inlining simple non-script path prefix matches from release_policy.sh would reduce helper dispatch in the main loop and improve classifier time.
asi.rollback_reason: Although it beat the original session baseline, it was worse than the current best kept state (71ms), so it does not justify replacing the existing implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the current best script-focused optimizations and look for remaining wins in changed_paths collection or Makefile-special handling that help the benchmarked scripts/* workload directly.
asi.lesson: Not every helper-inline translates into a win once the hottest path has already been simplified; measure against the current best, not just the original baseline.

description: Added an exact fast path for the common CI-router script files before the broader scripts/* patterns, but it still underperformed the current best classifier implementation.

asi.hypothesis: An exact fast path for the benchmarked CI-router script files would reduce pattern checks in classify_special_path and lower classifier time.
asi.rollback_reason: The added exact-match branch did not beat the current best result, so the extra special cases are not justified.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid adding benchmark-specific special cases unless they produce a clear win; the remaining meaningful work is likely still in changed_paths collection and git diff helpers.
asi.lesson: Micro-specialization for the benchmark path can add complexity without improving the already-optimized common script routing path.

description: Tried replacing the changed_paths sed/sort shell pipeline with a Python dedupe/sort pass, but the heredoc implementation consumed stdin and invalidated routing to noop.

asi.hypothesis: A single Python dedupe/sort pass could replace sed plus sort -u and reduce changed_paths collection overhead without changing semantics.
asi.rollback_reason: The implementation used `python3 - <<'PY'` in a pipeline, so Python read the script from stdin instead of the piped path list; changed_paths became empty and the benchmark workload collapsed to noop.
asi.next_action_hint: If revisiting Python-based changed_paths processing, pass code with `-c` or via a temp file so stdin remains available for path data.
asi.broken_tests: agent_ci_surface_contract_test (17 failures); benchmark surface_target_code fell from 2 to 0, so the measured classifier_ms was not comparable.
asi.lesson: Do not use heredoc-fed Python in a pipeline when the program must also consume stdin data; it silently invalidates the benchmark and routing semantics.

description: Re-tried changed_paths Python dedupe/sort using python3 -c so stdin stayed intact; it preserved semantics and checks passed, but still did not beat the current best classifier implementation.

asi.hypothesis: A Python dedupe/sort pass could replace sed plus sort -u with fewer shell processes and lower changed_paths overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: The corrected implementation preserved the workload and semantics, but its classifier time (72ms) remained slightly worse than the current best kept state (71ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep cf326ac as the best state; future wins likely require deeper changes to changed_paths collection or Makefile diff helpers, not more shell-to-Python swaps.
asi.lesson: A concept can be valid after a broken first attempt and still not be a net improvement; rerun cleanly, then compare against the current best rather than the original baseline.

description: Added an early scripts/* short-circuit in the docs-only scan, but it did not improve the classifier versus the current best implementation.

asi.hypothesis: A cheap scripts/* short-circuit in the docs-only phase would help the benchmark workload fail docs-only detection earlier and reduce total classifier work.
asi.rollback_reason: The optimization did not beat the current best result, so the extra special-case branch is not justified.
asi.next_action_hint: The remaining meaningful cost is likely outside the docs-only scan; focus on changed_paths collection or git diff helper cost rather than additional early exits here.
asi.lesson: Even semantically safe early exits are not worth keeping unless they produce a measurable win over the current best state.

description: Specialized Makefile diff collection inside the two Makefile helpers and removed the generic per-path diff helpers, but it still regressed versus the current best classifier implementation.

asi.hypothesis: Removing the generic diff-helper layer and scoping git diff collection directly to the Makefile-only helpers would reduce startup overhead on the hot scripts workload.
asi.rollback_reason: The specialized form did not outperform the current best kept implementation, so the refactor adds churn without measurable benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep cf326ac as the best state; remaining wins likely require a different approach to changed_paths collection or fewer git subprocesses overall, not more shell refactoring around rare Makefile logic.
asi.lesson: Refactoring rare-path helper structure does not necessarily help a scripts-focused workload when the underlying git subprocess count is unchanged.

description: Tried replacing separate staged+unstaged diff enumeration with a single git diff --name-only HEAD plus untracked files, but the classifier got slower on the benchmark workload.

asi.hypothesis: One git diff --name-only HEAD plus ls-files --others could replace two diff subprocesses while preserving the changed-path set for routing.
asi.rollback_reason: Although the path set matched for the current repo state, the combined diff command was slower in practice than the original staged+unstaged pair on this workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep cf326ac as the best state; future wins likely require reducing shell/process overhead in a different way or accepting that the remaining cost is dominated by git itself.
asi.lesson: Fewer subprocesses do not guarantee lower latency when the replacement command does more work internally.

description: Inlined classify_special_path into the main classification loop to remove one shell function call per path, but it still did not beat the current best classifier implementation.

asi.hypothesis: Removing the classify_special_path function call from the per-path loop would reduce shell dispatch overhead and improve classifier time.
asi.rollback_reason: Although this was directionally better than several recent attempts, it remained slower than the current best kept implementation (71ms), so the extra duplication is not justified.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep cf326ac as the best state; further wins likely require lower-level changes to git/path collection cost rather than more shell control-flow inlining.
asi.lesson: Function-call elimination helps only to a point; once the hot path is already simplified, further inlining can add duplication without delivering the top result.
